ACLRule.description:
A network access control rule on an infrastructure node.

Adapted from CybORG's subnet NACL model. Specifies directional
traffic rules between network segments.
ACLRule.title: ACLRule

Account.description:
A user account on a scenario node.

Distinct from OCR's ``Role`` model: roles map exercise participants
to VM logins for exercise access. Accounts describe the environment
state — what accounts attackers will encounter or exploit.
Account.title: Account

Agent.description:
An autonomous participant in the scenario.

Agents reference existing scenario elements:
- ``entity`` links to the entities section (team/role) and supplies
  identity and role per ADR-020
- ``starting_accounts`` links to the accounts section
- ``allowed_subnets`` links to infrastructure entries
- ``initial_knowledge`` references nodes and infrastructure
- ``starting_conditions`` links to the conditions section, giving the
  authoring surface a declarative hook for participant-relevant
  precondition checks (ACT-601)
- ``authority_anchors`` links to declared SDL elements (entities,
  relationships, content, etc.) that anchor what the participant is
  allowed or expected to do in scenario meaning (ACT-601, ADR-020)
- ``operating_scope`` links to targetable named scenario elements
  (subnets, hosts, services, content) defining where the participant
  may act or observe (ACT-601, ADR-020)
- ``observation_boundaries`` links to declared participant observation
  boundaries that define participant-specific projections of world and
  evidence state (SEM-208)
Agent.title: Agent

AssetValue.description: CIA triad asset valuation for scoring and risk assessment.
AssetValue.title: AssetValue

AssetValueLevel.description: CIA triad value level. Adapted from CybORG ConfidentialityValue.
AssetValueLevel.title: AssetValueLevel

Condition.description:
A monitoring check deployed to a VM.

Either ``command`` + ``interval`` or ``source`` must be set, not both.
Condition.title: Condition

ContainerImageBuildProvenance.description: Observed build/provenance facts for a custom container image artifact.
ContainerImageBuildProvenance.title: ContainerImageBuildProvenance

Content.description:
Data or files placed into a scenario node.

Supports three forms:
- ``file``: A single file at a specific path, optionally with inline text.
- ``dataset``: A collection of related items (emails, records, pcaps)
  delivered via a source package or listed as items.
- ``directory``: A directory structure placed at a destination path.
Content.title: Content

ContentItem.description: A single item within a dataset (e.g., one email, one record).
ContentItem.title: ContentItem

ContentType.description: Kind of content placed into a system.
ContentType.title: ContentType

DockerfileInstruction.description:
A structured record of one container build-recipe instruction.

The instruction is kept as a typed kind plus tokenized ``arguments``
rather than raw recipe text: raw Dockerfile/shell syntax can contain
``${...}`` strings that collide with ACES variable substitution.
DockerfileInstruction.title: DockerfileInstruction

DockerfileInstructionKind.description: Observed kind of a structured container build-recipe instruction.
DockerfileInstructionKind.title: DockerfileInstructionKind

Entity.description:
An organizational unit, team, or person in the exercise.

Entities can nest recursively. Flattened names use dot-notation:
``blue-team.bob`` refers to the ``bob`` entity nested inside
``blue-team``.
Entity.title: Entity

Evaluation.description: A group of metrics with a pass/fail threshold.
Evaluation.title: Evaluation

Event.description: A triggered action combining conditions and injects.
Event.title: Event

ExternalMappingLoss.description: Loss-labeled mapping from an external vocabulary to ACES semantics.
ExternalMappingLoss.title: ExternalMappingLoss

Feature.description: A software artifact, service, or configuration deployed to a VM.
Feature.title: Feature

FeatureType.description: Kind of feature deployed to a VM.
FeatureType.title: FeatureType

ImageAttestation.description:
Observed build-attestation availability and verification result.

Attestation *availability* (``status``) and *verification result*
(``verification``) are deliberately separate facts: a mutable local image
tag with no registry-visible OCI/in-toto/SLSA attestation is not the same
state as a failed verification (ADR-023 §5).
ImageAttestation.title: ImageAttestation

ImageAttestationStatus.description: Availability of a registry-visible build attestation for an image.
ImageAttestationStatus.title: ImageAttestationStatus

ImageAttestationType.description: Format of an observed image build attestation.
ImageAttestationType.title: ImageAttestationType

ImageBuildArg.description: An observed build argument with value-sensitivity classification.
ImageBuildArg.title: ImageBuildArg

ImageConfig.description:
Observed image-default configuration baked into the image artifact.

These are image defaults, distinct from runtime-effective container facts
recorded under ``Node.runtime.container``.
ImageConfig.title: ImageConfig

ImageCopiedSource.description: A source path copied into the image and its in-image destination.
ImageCopiedSource.title: ImageCopiedSource

ImageEnvironmentDefault.description: An image-default environment variable with sensitivity classification.
ImageEnvironmentDefault.title: ImageEnvironmentDefault

ImageLayer.description:
An observed layer in the image layer chain.

Empty/metadata-only layers (e.g. ``ENV`` instructions) legitimately carry
no ``digest``; the layer is still recorded for build-history correlation.
ImageLayer.title: ImageLayer

ImageSourceInput.description:
A source-package input and its source-to-runtime destination mapping.

``source_path`` uses the same source-path dialect as
``RuntimeFilesystemEntry.source_path`` so the build-time input and the
realized runtime entry can be correlated.
ImageSourceInput.title: ImageSourceInput

ImageVerificationStatus.description: Result of verifying an image's build attestation.
ImageVerificationStatus.title: ImageVerificationStatus

ImportDecl.description: A module import expanded before full semantic validation.
ImportDecl.title: ImportDecl

InfraNode.description:
Deployment parameters for a node.

Shorthand: ``node-name: 3`` (just the count).
Longhand: full dict with count, links, dependencies, properties, acls.
InfraNode.title: InfraNode

InitialKnowledge.description:
What an agent knows about the scenario at start time.

Adapted from CybORG's INT (Initial Network Topology).
Specifies which hosts, subnets, services, and accounts
the agent has knowledge of before the scenario begins.
InitialKnowledge.title: InitialKnowledge

Inject.description: An action injected between entities during an exercise.
Inject.title: Inject

Metric.description:
A scoring metric — either manual or conditional.

Manual metrics may require artifact submission. Conditional
metrics reference a condition that produces the score.
Metric.title: Metric

MinScore.description:
Pass/fail threshold — either absolute points or percentage.

Shorthand: ``min-score: 50`` (interpreted as percentage).
Longhand: ``min-score: {absolute: 50}`` or ``{percentage: 75}``.
MinScore.title: MinScore

ModuleDescriptor.description: Published module metadata for SDL composition.
ModuleDescriptor.title: ModuleDescriptor

Node.description:
A scenario node — either a VM or a Switch.

The ``type`` field determines which variant is active. VM fields
are only valid when type is VM; Switch nodes carry no extra data.
Node.title: Node

NodeType.description: Whether a node is a virtual machine or network switch.
NodeType.title: NodeType

OSFamily.description: Operating system family. Vocabulary from OCSF Device.os.
OSFamily.title: OSFamily

ObjectiveSuccess.description: Declarative success criteria for an objective.
ObjectiveSuccess.title: ObjectiveSuccess

ObjectiveWindow.description: Optional orchestration window constraining when an objective applies.
ObjectiveWindow.title: ObjectiveWindow

ParticipantActionContract.description: Governed semantic contract for one participant action name.
ParticipantActionContract.title: ParticipantActionContract

ParticipantActionEffect.description: Typed SEM-211 effect declaration on an action contract.
ParticipantActionEffect.title: ParticipantActionEffect

ParticipantActionGranularity.description: Behavioral granularity of a participant action contract.
ParticipantActionGranularity.title: ParticipantActionGranularity

ParticipantActionLifecycle.description: Governance lifecycle for participant action contracts.
ParticipantActionLifecycle.title: ParticipantActionLifecycle

ParticipantActionPrecondition.description: Typed SEM-211 applicability precondition on an action contract.
ParticipantActionPrecondition.title: ParticipantActionPrecondition

ParticipantBackendFailureMapping.description: Mapping from a backend diagnostic code to a portable SEM-211 failure.
ParticipantBackendFailureMapping.title: ParticipantBackendFailureMapping

ParticipantBackendTimingDisclosure.description: Backend timing realization disclosure for SEM-213 contracts.
ParticipantBackendTimingDisclosure.title: ParticipantBackendTimingDisclosure

ParticipantEffectClass.description: SEM-211 effect classes for participant action results.
ParticipantEffectClass.title: ParticipantEffectClass

ParticipantFailureClass.description: SEM-211 portable failure classes for participant action attempts.
ParticipantFailureClass.title: ParticipantFailureClass

ParticipantInformationBoundaryClass.description: SEM-210 classes for participant information-boundary objects.
ParticipantInformationBoundaryClass.title: ParticipantInformationBoundaryClass

ParticipantInteractionClass.description: SEM-209 interaction classes for multi-participant behavior.
ParticipantInteractionClass.title: ParticipantInteractionClass

ParticipantInteractionDeclaration.description: Declared interaction semantics for a participant action contract.
ParticipantInteractionDeclaration.title: ParticipantInteractionDeclaration

ParticipantPreconditionClass.description: SEM-211 precondition classes for participant action applicability.
ParticipantPreconditionClass.title: ParticipantPreconditionClass

ParticipantTemporalContract.description: Typed SEM-213 temporal contract for a participant action.
ParticipantTemporalContract.title: ParticipantTemporalContract

ParticipantTemporalEventPoint.description: Named participant event points used by temporal contracts.
ParticipantTemporalEventPoint.title: ParticipantTemporalEventPoint

ParticipantTemporalSupportMode.description: How a backend supports a temporal guarantee.
ParticipantTemporalSupportMode.title: ParticipantTemporalSupportMode

ParticipantViewDisposition.description: SEM-210 visibility disposition for one information object.
ParticipantViewDisposition.title: ParticipantViewDisposition

ParticipantViewHistoryEventType.description: Behavior-history event that realizes a SEM-210 visibility transition.
ParticipantViewHistoryEventType.title: ParticipantViewHistoryEventType

ParticipantViewRule.description: Visibility rule for one participant information-boundary object.
ParticipantViewRule.title: ParticipantViewRule

ParticipantViewTransition.description: Time-indexed SEM-210 transition over a participant view relation.
ParticipantViewTransition.title: ParticipantViewTransition

ParticipantViewTransitionKind.description: SEM-210 state transitions that alter participant visibility over time.
ParticipantViewTransitionKind.title: ParticipantViewTransitionKind

PasswordStrength.description: How resistant the account password is to cracking.
PasswordStrength.title: PasswordStrength

Relationship.description:
A typed directed edge between two named scenario elements.

Source and target can reference any named element in the scenario:
nodes, features, accounts, entities, or infrastructure entries.
The validator checks that both endpoints resolve.

The ``properties`` dict carries type-specific metadata (e.g.,
``trust_type: parent-child`` for AD trusts, ``protocol: SAML``
for federation). It's a flat dict rather than typed sub-models
because relationship properties vary widely and we don't want
to gate expressiveness on pre-modeling every variant.
Relationship.title: Relationship

RelationshipType.description: How two scenario elements relate to each other.
RelationshipType.title: RelationshipType

Resources.description: Compute resources for a VM node.
Resources.title: Resources

Role.description:
A named role on a VM with optional entity assignments.

Shorthand: ``admin: "username"`` (just the username string).
Longhand: ``admin: {username: "admin", entities: ["blue-team.bob"]}``.
Role.title: Role

RuntimeCapabilityPolicy.description: Linux/container capability policy observed for a runtime node.
RuntimeCapabilityPolicy.title: RuntimeCapabilityPolicy

RuntimeConfiguration.description: Observed runtime configuration facts attached to a VM node.
RuntimeConfiguration.title: RuntimeConfiguration

RuntimeContainerConfiguration.description: Observed container runtime host and security configuration facts.
RuntimeContainerConfiguration.title: RuntimeContainerConfiguration

RuntimeControlInterface.description: A non-network local control API exposed inside a runtime node.
RuntimeControlInterface.title: RuntimeControlInterface

RuntimeControlInterfaceKind.description: Path-local control interface shape observed at runtime.
RuntimeControlInterfaceKind.title: RuntimeControlInterfaceKind

RuntimeDependencyManifest.description: A dependency manifest visible in the realized runtime artifact.
RuntimeDependencyManifest.title: RuntimeDependencyManifest

RuntimeDeviceMapping.description: A host device mapping observed in runtime configuration.
RuntimeDeviceMapping.title: RuntimeDeviceMapping

RuntimeEnvironmentValueClassification.description: Sensitivity classification for an observed runtime environment value.
RuntimeEnvironmentValueClassification.title: RuntimeEnvironmentValueClassification

RuntimeEnvironmentVariable.description: Observed runtime environment variable with provenance and sensitivity.
RuntimeEnvironmentVariable.title: RuntimeEnvironmentVariable

RuntimeEnvironmentVariableProvenance.description: Origin class for an observed runtime environment variable.
RuntimeEnvironmentVariableProvenance.title: RuntimeEnvironmentVariableProvenance

RuntimeExtraHost.description: An observed extra host mapping in runtime configuration.
RuntimeExtraHost.title: RuntimeExtraHost

RuntimeFilesystemEntry.description: A filesystem entry observed inside a runtime node or container asset.
RuntimeFilesystemEntry.title: RuntimeFilesystemEntry

RuntimeFilesystemEntryType.description: Observed type of a runtime filesystem inventory entry.
RuntimeFilesystemEntryType.title: RuntimeFilesystemEntryType

RuntimeFilesystemStability.description: Stability class for an observed runtime filesystem or mount fact.
RuntimeFilesystemStability.title: RuntimeFilesystemStability

RuntimeHealthObservation.description: Observed runtime health status and healthcheck log facts.
RuntimeHealthObservation.title: RuntimeHealthObservation

RuntimeHealthStatus.description: Observed health status for a runtime node or container.
RuntimeHealthStatus.title: RuntimeHealthStatus

RuntimeHealthcheckLog.description: An observed runtime healthcheck log entry.
RuntimeHealthcheckLog.title: RuntimeHealthcheckLog

RuntimeIdentityProvenance.description: Origin class for an observed local identity record.
RuntimeIdentityProvenance.title: RuntimeIdentityProvenance

RuntimeLocalGroup.description: A local group record observed in a node's identity database (/etc/group).
RuntimeLocalGroup.title: RuntimeLocalGroup

RuntimeLocalIdentityInventory.description: Observed local identity database (users, groups, sudo rules) for a node.
RuntimeLocalIdentityInventory.title: RuntimeLocalIdentityInventory

RuntimeLocalUser.description: A local user record observed in a node's identity database (/etc/passwd).
RuntimeLocalUser.title: RuntimeLocalUser

RuntimeMount.description: A filesystem mount observed on a runtime node.
RuntimeMount.title: RuntimeMount

RuntimeMountPropagation.description: Portable propagation mode for a runtime filesystem mount.
RuntimeMountPropagation.title: RuntimeMountPropagation

RuntimeMountSourceKind.description: Portable source kind for a runtime filesystem mount.
RuntimeMountSourceKind.title: RuntimeMountSourceKind

RuntimeNamespaceConfiguration.description: Observed namespace modes for a runtime node or container.
RuntimeNamespaceConfiguration.title: RuntimeNamespaceConfiguration

RuntimeNetworkBackendDetail.description:
Observed backend network driver and IPAM detail for an endpoint's network.

``driver_options`` and ``ipam_options`` are the bounded extension seam for
backend-native key/value facts; raw backend inspect payloads are not stored.
RuntimeNetworkBackendDetail.title: RuntimeNetworkBackendDetail

RuntimeNetworkDriver.description: Observed backend network driver class for a realized network.
RuntimeNetworkDriver.title: RuntimeNetworkDriver

RuntimeNetworkEndpoint.description:
An observed container network endpoint (per-network attachment).

``network`` references a declared switch-backed infrastructure network by
name. Per-network ``aliases``, observed ``dns_names``, and backend-generated
``generated_dns_names`` are kept as three distinct lists; generated names
are not stable scenario identity.
RuntimeNetworkEndpoint.title: RuntimeNetworkEndpoint

RuntimeNetworkIdStability.description:
Stability class for a backend-generated network or endpoint identifier.

Distinct from :class:`RuntimeFilesystemStability`: filesystem stability and
backend-identifier stability are different concepts (ADR-025).
RuntimeNetworkIdStability.title: RuntimeNetworkIdStability

RuntimeNetworkRealization.description:
Observed container network realization facts for a node.

``hostname`` and ``domainname`` are the container's network identity facts,
kept distinct from per-network aliases and DNS names.
RuntimeNetworkRealization.title: RuntimeNetworkRealization

RuntimeOperationalPolicy.description: Observed restart and resource-limit policy for a runtime node.
RuntimeOperationalPolicy.title: RuntimeOperationalPolicy

RuntimePackage.description: A package observed in a runtime image or node.
RuntimePackage.title: RuntimePackage

RuntimePackageVulnerabilityFinding.description: A scanner-derived CVE/advisory finding for an observed package.
RuntimePackageVulnerabilityFinding.title: RuntimePackageVulnerabilityFinding

RuntimeProcessIdentity.description: Observed process identity for a runtime node.
RuntimeProcessIdentity.title: RuntimeProcessIdentity

RuntimeProcessRole.description: Observed role of a process in a runtime process set.
RuntimeProcessRole.title: RuntimeProcessRole

RuntimePublishedPort.description:
An observed host-published port binding for a container endpoint.

Host IP, host port, container port, and protocol are kept distinct; this is
a runtime/host exposure fact, not a node service declaration (ADR-025).
RuntimePublishedPort.title: RuntimePublishedPort

RuntimeResourceLimits.description: Observed runtime/cgroup resource limits for a node.
RuntimeResourceLimits.title: RuntimeResourceLimits

RuntimeRestartPolicy.description: Portable restart policy classification observed at runtime.
RuntimeRestartPolicy.title: RuntimeRestartPolicy

RuntimeSensitivityClassification.description: Sensitivity/redaction class for observed runtime facts.
RuntimeSensitivityClassification.title: RuntimeSensitivityClassification

RuntimeSudoPrincipalKind.description: Kind of principal a sudo rule grants privilege to.
RuntimeSudoPrincipalKind.title: RuntimeSudoPrincipalKind

RuntimeSudoRule.description:
An observed sudo/sudoers privilege grant.

The portable model is the structured principal/run-as/command scope.
``raw_entry`` may carry the original sudoers line only as optional
descriptive evidence; ``command_redacted`` marks a rule whose command
scope was withheld because it carried sensitive arguments.
RuntimeSudoRule.title: RuntimeSudoRule

Script.description:
A timed sequence of events.

Time values are human-readable duration strings parsed to seconds.
Script.title: Script

ServicePort.description: A network service exposed by a node. From OCSF NetworkEndpoint.
ServicePort.title: ServicePort

SimpleProperties.description: Network properties for a switch/subnet: CIDR, gateway, and flags.
SimpleProperties.title: SimpleProperties

Source.description:
Provider-neutral artifact reference.

Shorthand: ``source: "package-name"`` (version defaults to ``"*"``).
Longhand: ``source: {name: "package-name", version: "1.2.3"}``.
Source.title: Source

Story.description: Top-level exercise orchestration — a group of scripts.
Story.title: Story

TLO.description: Training Learning Objective — linked to an evaluation.
TLO.title: TLO

Variable.description:
A named variable for scenario parameterization.

Variables define configurable parameters with types, defaults,
and optional value constraints. They're referenced in other
sections via ``${variable_name}`` syntax.
Variable.title: Variable

Vulnerability.description: A named vulnerability with CWE classification.
Vulnerability.title: Vulnerability

Workflow.description: A declarative experiment control graph over objectives.
Workflow.title: Workflow

WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy.description: How primary workflow status should treat compensation failures.
WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy.title: WorkflowCompensationFailurePolicy

WorkflowCompensationTrigger.description: Terminal workflow reasons that may trigger compensation.
WorkflowCompensationTrigger.title: WorkflowCompensationTrigger

WorkflowPredicate.description: Branch predicate over runtime evaluation data and prior step state.
WorkflowPredicate.title: WorkflowPredicate

WorkflowStep.description: A named workflow step with explicit portable control semantics.
WorkflowStep.title: WorkflowStep

WorkflowStepOutcome.description: Portable workflow-visible outcomes emitted by executable steps.
WorkflowStepOutcome.title: WorkflowStepOutcome

WorkflowStepStateRef.description: Predicate reference to previously observed workflow step state.
WorkflowStepStateRef.title: WorkflowStepStateRef

WorkflowStepType.description: Control-flow node types for declarative experiment workflows.
WorkflowStepType.title: WorkflowStepType

WorkflowSwitchCase.description: One ordered branch case within a ``switch`` workflow step.
WorkflowSwitchCase.title: WorkflowSwitchCase

description: Scenario with all `${var}` references resolved to concrete values.
title: InstantiatedScenario
